promise.quote: Here, the view defines the experience. Our guests can be assured that every harbourfront moment, whether from a private suite or the infinity spa pools, is as breathtaking as promised.
promise.name: Michel Chertouh
promise.title: Managing Director

moments.text: Symphony of Lights from the Lobby Lounge — cocktails as the skyline ignites (Bank of China Tower, IFC, The Peak, Observation Wheel in alignment)
Sunrise from the CEO Suite's harbour-facing terrace
New Year's fireworks & seasonal drone shows through the Lobby Lounge windows
First-light breakfast at Harbourside as the ferries cross the quiet harbour
The secluded east-facing infinity spa pools opening onto Victoria Harbour

best_picks.description: A two-storey rooftop suite with a 2,500 sq ft terrace, private infinity pool, and a 270-degree harbour panorama
Indoor-outdoor living anchored by a harbour-facing infinity daybed and a 3,200 sq ft terrace built for sunrise
Two levels with an entertainment room and a generous terrace; panoramic baths facing the harbour
1,075 sq ft, capturing the skyline from multiple angles

public_spaces.name: Lai Ching Heen
public_spaces.note: Michelin Cantonese on jade-inlaid settings, Victoria Harbour through the windows

public_spaces.name: Nobu Hong Kong
public_spaces.note: Japanese-Peruvian classics — black cod miso, toro tartare — against the harbour

public_spaces.name: The Steak House
public_spaces.note: Charcoal-grilled cuts; a handful of tables look straight out over the harbour

public_spaces.name: Harbourside
public_spaces.note: All-day dining and buffet with nearly every table on the harbourfront

public_spaces.name: The Lobby Lounge
public_spaces.note: Floor-to-ceiling skyline views; a front-row seat for the Symphony of Lights and fireworks

public_spaces.name: Pool Terrace
public_spaces.note: An outdoor pool plus secluded east-facing infinity spa pools opening onto the harbour
